# Qorstack Report

> Self-hosted document API. Upload a Word/Excel template, POST JSON, get a PDF/DOCX/XLSX back. MIT core; optional Pro license for PDF password & watermark. Runs entirely on your infrastructure via Docker.

It is template-based document generation: stored templates + JSON merge (variables, tables, images, QR/barcode) + API-key auth + result storage + generation history. It uses Gotenberg internally for the DOCX→PDF step, but unlike raw Gotenberg it manages templates, auth, storage, and history — use Qorstack when you merge JSON into stored templates and keep records; use Gotenberg directly only for stateless one-off file→PDF conversion.

## Quickstart

- Deploy: copy `selfhost/docker-compose.yml` + `.env.example`, then `cp .env.example .env && docker compose up -d`. Web UI at http://localhost:3000 (admin/admin), API at http://localhost:8080.
- Smoke test: `bash selfhost/verify.sh` (or `make verify`).
- Generate: `POST http://localhost:8080/render/word/template` with header `X-API-Key: <key>` and body `{ "templateKey": "...", "fileType": "pdf", "replace": { ... } }`. Returns `{ jobId, downloadUrl, status }`.

## Docs

- AGENTS.md — full agent integration guide (deploy, API key, render, when-vs-Gotenberg)
- examples/invoice/ — runnable end-to-end demo (DOCX template + JSON + script → PDF)
- mcp/ — MCP server exposing list/get/generate tools (preferred for MCP-capable agents)
- /docs (web UI) — human + API reference with request shapes
- sdk/ — typed Node.js and .NET SDKs generated from the OpenAPI document
- /self-host (web UI) and selfhost/ — self-host guide and env reference

## Notes

- Auth header is `X-API-Key`; `/render/*` returns 401 without a valid key.
- Default admin user and `change-me-*` secrets are localhost-only — rotate before exposing.
- Pin versions with `REPORT_API_TAG` / `REPORT_WEB_TAG` in `.env` (default `latest`).
